title: Merchandisers
description: Purchase farm products either for further processing or resale. Includes tree farm contractors, grain brokers and market operators, grain buyers, and tobacco buyers. May negotiate contracts. Buy merchandise or commodities, other than farm products, for resale to consumers at the wholesale or retail level, including both durable and nondurable goods. Analyze past buying trends, sales records, price, and quality of merchandise to determine value and yield. Select, order, and authorize payment for merchandise according to contractual agreements. May conduct meetings with sales personnel and introduce new products. May negotiate contracts. Includes assistant wholesale and retail buyers of nonfarm products. Purchase machinery, equipment, tools, parts, supplies, or services necessary for the operation of an establishment. Purchase raw or semifinished materials for manufacturing. May negotiate contracts. (Based on 18 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Visit venues to demonstrate products or services and answer questions from potential customers.
Examine, select, order, or purchase merchandise consistent with quality, quantity, and specification requirements.
Negotiate prices, contract terms, or transportation arrangements with suppliers for merchandise.
Analyze sales records, trends, or conditions to anticipate consumer purchasing patterns and needed inventory.
Purchase farm products such as grains and milk for further processing or resale, following contractual agreements.
Maintain records of business transactions and inventory, reporting data to companies or government agencies as needed.
Collaborate with vendors to acquire or develop desired products.
Authorize payment for purchased merchandise and manage invoicing processes.
Conduct meetings to introduce new merchandise and provide product information to staff and customers.
Monitor supply and demand changes, tracking market conditions and price trends to inform buying decisions.
Research suppliers based on factors such as price, quality, and reliability to identify optimal purchasing options.
Track the flow of work forms, including in-house data flow or electronic forms transfer.
Train staff on technical procedures or software program usage.
Supervise the work of data management project staff while ensuring productivity or quality compliance.
Prepare data analysis listings, performance reports, and documentation of database changes.

Process clinical data involving receipt, entry, verification, or filing of information.
Analyze or manipulate bioinformatics data using software packages or statistical applications.
Collect business intelligence data and monitor current or potential customers using business intelligence tools.
Communicate with customers, suppliers, or competitors to stay updated on industry trends and marketing strategies.
Conduct market research analysis to identify trends and factors affecting product demand and strategies.

Prepare reports summarizing findings, illustrating data graphically, and translating complex findings into written text.
Measure and assess customer satisfaction, transforming findings into actionable insights for marketing.

Document specifications for information technology reports and dashboards to ensure accurate development of outputs.
Review advertising trends, consumer surveys, and other data regarding marketing of goods to determine ways to promote products.
Develop advertising campaigns for clients, coordinating with an advertising agency's creative director and art director.
Write advertising material for publication, broadcast, or internet media to promote the sale of goods and services.
Write articles, bulletins, sales letters, and other marketing and promotional material targeted to clients' needs.
